what_it_does: Records a noticeboard policy item stating that community plugins should generally not be adopted as a category; any use must be case-by-case and read-only opportunistic, because most plugins assume human-authored wikilink-connected vaults rather than typed-address edges and many write data.
observations: Title is 'P12 · 9. Community plugins (broad) — MOSTLY NONE; case-by-case.'
It is a current block sourced from claude_code in the dragnet-development channel.
It links to board://item-3480c157 via part_of.
The body explains that most plugins assume human-authored, wikilink-connected vaults and do not read typed-address edges.
Many plugins write, which is treated as a risk.
It allows opportunistic read-only mining, giving a calendar/timeline plugin reading the created field as an example.
It explicitly says never treat community plugins as a category-level adopt.
standouts: Mismatch between plugin assumptions (wikilink vaults) and system model (typed-address edges).
Read-only opportunistic use is the only endorsed pattern.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Markdown body with a numbered section heading.
References other board items with board:// URIs.
Title prefixed with 'P12 · 9.' indicating a position in a larger list.
concerns: 'MOSTLY NONE' is a broad prohibition without documented criteria or exceptions.
The case-by-case process is not defined.
The calendar/timeline example is vague about how integration would work.
No list of evaluated plugins or specific write risks.
notes: A conservative noticeboard guideline that rejects blanket adoption of community plugins, allowing only read-only, opportunistic integrations because most plugins target wikilink-based vaults and may write.
questions: What are the exact criteria for a case-by-case plugin approval?
Which write behaviors are considered unsafe?
How would a plugin be adapted to read typed-address edges?
What is the parent item-3480c157 and how does this item relate to it?
What does 'P12 · 9' signify in the numbering scheme?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the title and body both state a mostly-none, case-by-case policy on community plugins and give the rationale.
apparent_themes: Avoiding assumptions of human-authored wikilink vaults.
intention_signals: To establish a cautious, principle-based boundary around third-party plugins, permitting only read-only, edge-compatible uses while discouraging wholesale adoption.
novelty: It explicitly frames the adoption decision around the mismatch between plugins built for wikilink-connected human vaults and a typed-address ledger, and treats community plugins as a long-tail to mine rather than a category to adopt.
contribution: A cautionary principle and a read-only opportunistic-use pattern for integrating community plugins, plus the rationale that plugin assumptions about wikilink vaults and write behavior make broad adoption unsafe.
summary_for_embedding: A current noticeboard item in the dragnet-development channel, part of item-3480c157, recording a mostly-none, case-by-case policy on community plugins because they assume wikilink-connected human vaults and often write, with read-only opportunistic use such as reading the created field allowed.
intention_for_embedding: To set a conservative adoption stance for community plugins in a typed-address ledger, allowing only read-only, edge-compatible opportunistic integrations.
